text: The training of deep learning (DL) models is facing a grand challenge in this era known as data scarcity. In fact, the training of many DL models needs to rely heavily on a large amount of data in order to achieve a satisfactory performance, while there are so many applications having limited amount of data. Furthermore, the data required for the training of DL models usually needs to be labeled by humans with a very deep knowledge. As a result, the annotation process may become a bottleneck in many applications. In fact, the majority of the DL frameworks require a large amount of labeled training data to learn representations automatically. In addition, the amount of data required for the training of the DL models should be large enough to achieve a satisfactory performance, while this performance is application-dependent. In summary, having enough training data is the first step to design a trustworthy deep learning application. In this survey, we aim to provide a holistic survey on the existing works used to train deep learning models in order to overcome three major challenges of data scarcity, which are: training a deep learning model by using limited training data, imbalanced data, and lack of generalization to the unseen data in the test phase. We start this survey by listing the learning techniques, followed by listing the types of deep learning models. After that, we present the state-of-the-art solutions such as Transfer Learning (TL), Self-Supervised Learning (SSL), Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Model Architecture (MA), Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN), and Deep Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (DeepSMOTE) that are proposed in the literature to train a trustworthy deep learning model given limited amount of training data. Finally, we present some tips for data acquisition needed before the training, and also recommend some ways to design a reliable training dataset. We conclude this survey by listing the numerous applications where the data scarcity is still a barrier, and we also list the various methods that can be employed to train a reliable deep learning model given the limited data in these applications which include Electromagnetic Imaging (EMI), Civil Structural Health Monitoring, Medical imaging, Meteorology, Wireless Communications, Fluid Mechanics, Microelectromechanical system, and Cybersecurity. To the authors’ knowledge, this is the first survey to provide a comprehensive overview of the existing works proposed to combat the data scarcity issue for the deep learning applications.
